catalyst used and its specification: carbon-supported iron carbide, specifically Fe-Fe3C/C . These catalysts were synthesized using a high-temperature solid-phase method under a reducing atmosphere (10% H2 / 90% Ar) Structural Composition: Characterization techniques such as X-ray diffraction (XRD) and Raman spectroscopy revealed that the catalysts consist of graphitized and amorphous carbon encapsulating iron carbide nanocrystals and metal iron nanoclusters Performance in Lignin Conversion: The Fe-Fe3C/C catalysts demonstrated high selectivity for aromatic monomers, achieving an 86 wt% selectivity for guaiacol and its alkyl derivatives at a 48 wt% yield during optimal experimental conditions Mechanism of Action: The catalysts function by utilizing acid sites to adsorb and break C-O bonds, while metal Fe acts as a hydrogen source to promote hydrogenation processes. This dual functionality enhances the overall efficiency of lignin depolymerization
solvent used: Ethanol, ethanol is chosen as the solvent for its dual role as a good solvent for lignin fragments and a hydrogen donor, which together enhance the efficiency and selectivity of the lignin depolymerization process.
product yield %: Guaiacol and 4-alkyl guaiacols: 86 wt% , Lignin Oil Yield:48 wt% , Solid Product Yield: from 48.84% to 57.15%
selectivity %: high selectivity of 86 wt% for guaiacol and its alkylated derivatives
Conversion rate: the Kraft lignin depolymerization process achieved a conversion rate of 48 wt% with a high selectivity of 86 wt% for guaiacol and 4-alkyl guaiacols, showcasing the effectiveness of the Fe-C-700 catalyst in this reaction.

catalyst used and its specification: NiRu/Al₂O₃, which is a bimetallic catalyst composed of nickel (Ni) and ruthenium (Ru) supported on aluminum oxide (Al₂O₃) .  The catalyst exhibits a mesoporous structure with pore sizes concentrated in the range of 5-20 nm. This pore structure facilitates the mass transfer of enzymatic hydrolysis lignin (EHL) fragments during the reaction, improving the overall efficiency of the catalytic process
product yield %: 58.1% for the SP (soluble portion) and 38.1% for monomeric phenols in the SP cat.
selectivity %: monomeric phenols - 38.1% in the soluble portion
Conversion rate: the paper does not provide a specific numerical conversion rate, the reported SP yield of 58.1% under optimal conditions suggests a high level of conversion of EHL into valuable phenolic products.

catalyst used and its specification: bimetallic catalyst  NiCu/C. synthesized through an impregnation method, the NiCu/C catalyst achieves a lignin conversion rate of 92.4% and a monomer yield of 17.7 wt%. specific surface area of 1066.6 m²/g , The total pore volume of the NiCu/C catalyst is 0.84 cm³/g ,
selectivity %: Guaiacol: 9.3 wt% , Phenols: 6.4 wt% , Other aromatic products: 1.9 wt% , Aromatic hydrogenated products: 0.1 wt%
reactor used: batch reactor or a high-pressure reactor
Conversion rate: the NiCu/C catalyst demonstrates a high conversion rate of 92.4% in the hydrogenolysis of lignin, making it a promising option for producing aromatic monomers.

catalyst used and its specification:
1. Palladium (Pd)
Supported on activated biochar (ABC).
This catalyst serves as the primary active metal in the process.
2. 5% Zirconium (Zr)
Also supported on ABC.
Contributes to the catalytic activity and selectivity towards phenolic monomers.
3. 5% Tungsten (W)
Supported on ABC.
Plays a role in enhancing the selectivity of the hydrogenation process.
4. 5% Molybdenum (Mo) Supported on ABC.
Inhibits the hydrogenation of aliphatic Cα=Cβ in lignin, leading to specific product formation like t-isoeugenol.
5.  Catalyst Performance:
The selectivity to C9 monomeric phenols followed this trend:
2Pd-5Mo/ABC: 57.3% selectivity
2Pd-5Zr/ABC: 49.2% selectivity
2Pd-5W/ABC: 45% selectivity
2Pd/ABC: 42.9% selectivity
The maximum yield of C9 phenolic monomers achieved was approximately 22 wt%.
The fractional conversion of lignin with these catalysts ranged from 67-69%
preparation temperature for catalyst: Activation Temperature of the catalyst were 300 degree celcius and the Reduction Temperature of the palladium catalysts was carried out by increasing the temperature to 900 °C during temperature programmed reduction (TPR) experiments, after initially cooling to 100 °C
solvent used: The solvent used in the process was methanol. Methanol served as the medium for the reaction, facilitating the dissolution of lignin and the products formed during hydrogenolysis
product yield %: The maximum yield of C9 phenolic monomers achieved in this study was approximately 22 wt%. This yield indicates the efficiency of the catalysts in converting lignin into valuable phenolic products
selectivity %: The selectivity to C9 monomeric phenols followed a specific trend based on the catalyst used  2Pd-5Mo/ABC: 57.3% , 2Pd-5Zr/ABC: 49.2% , 2Pd-5W/ABC: 45% , 2Pd/ABC: 42.9% This data highlights how different catalysts influence the selectivity towards desired products
reactor used: The reaction was conducted in a 100 mL autoclave made of Inconel alloy. This type of reactor is suitable for high-pressure and high-temperature reactions, which are essential for the hydrogenolysis process
Conversion rate: the conversion rates for lignin depolymerization in this study show a significant improvement with the use of catalysts, achieving a maximum conversion of 31% without a catalyst and up to 42.2 wt% of phenolic monomer yield with optimized conditions and catalysts.

catalyst used and its specification:
spherical NiCo-based Metal-Organic Framework (MOF) catalyst, Composition: The catalyst is composed of Nickel (Ni) and Cobalt (Co) in varying ratios. For instance, the Ni:Co weight ratios are reported as:
Ni3Co1/C: 2.91:1
Ni1Co1/C: 1.01:1
Ni3Co1/MOF: 2.89:1
Ni1Co1/MOF: 0.98:1 surface area : 135.68 m²/g to 159.75 m²/g, depending on the Co content  particle size : Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) and was found to decrease from 8.6 nm to 5.0 nm with the introduction of Co
preparation temperature for catalyst: high temperature under a nitrogen atmosphere for 3 hours
temperature(process): The catalytic hydrogenolysis process was conducted at a temperature of 280 ℃. This temperature was maintained for a duration of 6 hours during the reaction
pressure(process): The reactor was pressurized with nitrogen to 2.0 MPa at room temperature before heating, ensuring an inert atmosphere during the reaction
product yield %: the catalytic hydrogenolysis process achieves a lignin oil yield of 75.6 wt%
selectivity %: demonstrates high selectivity for ethylcyclohexane and cyclohexanol, with yields greater than 90 mol%.
Conversion rate: The paper indicates that the catalytic conversion rate of lignin using the optimal Ni3Co1/C catalyst significantly improved from 18% to 99%. This dramatic increase highlights the effectiveness of the bimetallic catalyst in enhancing the hydrogenolysis process of lignin model compounds and enzymatic lignin under nitrogen atmosphere
